name: Marriott Bonvoy Bevy® Card
effective_cost: Depends entirely on hotel loyalty — no statement credits

verdict.text: Thin on credits — hard to justify without heavy Marriott spend

earn_rates.category: Restaurants worldwide & U.S. supermarkets (combined $15,000/yr cap, then 2×)

earn_note: Unlike the Brilliant, dining and groceries share one combined $15,000/yr cap rather than separate ones — easier to blow through if you use both categories heavily.

redemption_options.method: Transfer to airline miles (3:1, +5,000 bonus per 60,000 transferred)

points.note: Same Bonvoy currency and redemption math as the Brilliant — the Bevy just has far fewer ways to earn its fee back.

transfer_partners.highlight: Marriott Bonvoy transfers to 40+ airline programs at 3:1, with a 5,000-mile bonus for every 60,000 points transferred — same chart as every other Bonvoy card.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: No confirmed recent changes to the airline transfer chart.

credits.name: Free Night Award
credits.subtitle: up to 50,000-point category · after $15,000/yr spend
credits.description: One free night award earned after spending $15,000 on the card in a calendar year, redeemable at hotels with a redemption level at or under 50,000 Marriott Bonvoy points.
tips: A real spend threshold, not a renewal-based freebie like the Brilliant's — don't count this unless you're already going to hit $15,000/yr.
$250 here is a conservative mid-range estimate for a capped-value redemption.

insurance.detail: Up to $50,000, secondary, no liability coverage
+1 year on warranties of 5 years or less (US purchases)

protection_note: No trip cancellation/interruption coverage, and the 12-hour trip delay trigger is weaker than the Brilliant's 6-hour window. This card's protections sit closer to a no-fee card than a $250-fee one.
rental_note: Secondary and capped lower than the Brilliant's rental coverage — put rentals on a card with primary coverage if you rent often.

status_perks.name: Marriott Bonvoy Gold Elite Status
status_perks.note: Complimentary with the card — 25% bonus points on stays and space-available upgrades where available. One tier below the Brilliant's Platinum status. Plus 15 Elite Night Credits/year toward the next tier.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Zero FX fees on international spend.

additional_cards.title: Additional Cards

benefits.text: Earns Marriott Bonvoy points on their spend (into your account)
Does not get their own Free Night Award or Elite status

additional_cards.note: Additional cards are free — a way to pool household spend toward the $15,000 Free Night Award threshold.
